issue.title: Bolin Creek water quality concerns
issue.summary: Residents along Bolin Creek report discoloration and odor after recent storm runoff. The town board scheduled a public works briefing; neighborhood associations are organizing for answers.

ranked_events.target_voters: Soft precincts CB1, NC, and DA in Carrboro; environmentally engaged homeowners and renters near creek corridors
talking_points: Commit to transparent water testing and public reporting timelines
Fund stormwater infrastructure upgrades along the Bolin Creek corridor
Establish a resident advisory panel for creek-adjacent neighborhoods
ranked_events.rationale: Highest-salience issue in the district with an active neighborhood coalition. Rain forecast makes an indoor listening session the credible, weather-safe format this week.
ranked_events.draft_outreach: Carrboro neighbors: join us Saturday for a community listening session on Bolin Creek water quality. Share what you're seeing, ask questions, and help shape our clean-water priorities. Indoor event — Carrboro Century Center, 2pm. RSVP link in bio.

issue.title: Caswell County school funding shortfall
issue.summary: The Caswell County Board of Education faces a budget gap heading into fall, with proposed cuts to arts and after-school programs. Parent groups and teachers' associations are mobilizing ahead of the July budget vote.

ranked_events.target_voters: Parents and educators in Caswell precincts YANC, PELH, and PROVI; soft turnout among rural families outside Yanceyville
talking_points: Protect classroom funding and after-school programs
Oppose cuts that hit arts and vocational education first
Push for transparent budget hearings before the July vote
ranked_events.rationale: School funding is the top Caswell County conversation with a hard budget deadline. Clear weather supports an outdoor rally that draws families and local press.
ranked_events.draft_outreach: Caswell County families: our schools need us before the July budget vote. Join us at the Yanceyville Town Square this Sunday for a rally to protect classroom funding and after-school programs. Bring signs, bring neighbors.

issue.title: Northside affordable housing crunch
issue.summary: Rising rents and limited inventory are squeezing longtime Northside residents. A proposed infill development has sparked debate over displacement versus new affordable units.

ranked_events.target_voters: Renters and longtime homeowners in Northside precincts NS, TH1, and EH; seniors facing displacement pressure
talking_points: Require affordable-unit set-asides in new infill developments
Expand tenant protections and anti-displacement resources
Invest in community land trusts for Northside
ranked_events.rationale: Housing costs are climbing fastest in Northside, where turnout opportunity is high among renters who rarely hear from campaigns. Thunderstorms favor an indoor town hall.
ranked_events.draft_outreach: Northside: rents are up and options are down. Come to a town hall on affordable housing and the proposed infill project — your voice shapes what gets built. Hargraves Community Center, Thursday 6:30pm.

issue.title: Rural broadband gaps in northern Caswell
issue.summary: Farmers and small businesses around Milton report unreliable internet after a provider delayed fiber expansion. County commissioners are weighing a public-private partnership to close coverage gaps.

ranked_events.target_voters: Rural voters in Caswell precincts MILT, LEAS, and STON; small-business owners dependent on reliable connectivity
talking_points: Accelerate county fiber partnerships for underserved rural roads
Treat broadband as essential infrastructure for farms and small business
Demand accountability from providers that miss expansion deadlines
ranked_events.rationale: Northern Caswell voters feel overlooked on infrastructure; an outdoor event in Milton signals the campaign shows up beyond Yanceyville. Partly cloudy forecast is workable for a park pavilion format.
ranked_events.draft_outreach: Northern Caswell: tired of spotty internet holding back your farm or business? Meet us at the Milton community park to talk rural broadband and what the county can do now. Saturday morning, coffee provided.

issue.title: Chapel Hill Transit route cuts proposed
issue.summary: Chapel Hill Transit proposed eliminating two crosstown routes to close a budget shortfall. Riders and transit advocates are pushing back, citing disproportionate impact on working families and UNC staff.

weather.summary: Hot and humid, heat index near 100

ranked_events.venue_suggestion: Chapel Hill Public Library meeting room
ranked_events.target_voters: Transit-dependent riders in Chapel Hill precincts ES, CO, and EA1; UNC staff and service workers on proposed cut routes
talking_points: Oppose route cuts that hit crosstown workers hardest
Fund Chapel Hill Transit through fair-share partner contributions
Center rider testimony in any service-reduction decision
ranked_events.rationale: Route cuts are imminent and riders are already organizing. An indoor forum near downtown stops makes participation easy for the people most affected.
ranked_events.draft_outreach: Chapel Hill riders: proposed transit cuts would hit crosstown routes first. Join a rider forum at the Chapel Hill Public Library to share your story and fight for reliable transit. Wednesday 5:30pm.
